commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Contributor-supplied real runtime proof is still required, and maintainers need either the non-ok SSE fix or an explicit partial-hardening decision before merge.; Needs attention: Security review found an incomplete DoS mitigation: non-ok SSE responses remain unbounded through the SDK error parser.; Review finding: [P1] Bound non-ok SSE bodies too — src/agents/sdk-bounded-fetch.ts:38-39 (sha=dbde14df421745b524c7d16ff421aa41276981be)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a shared `createSdkBoundedFetch` helper, tests it, and injects it into both Azure OpenAI Responses SDK client constructors to cap successful SSE response bodies at 16 MiB.

PR surface: Source +104, Tests +200. Total +304 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection. Current main constructs Azure Responses SDK clients without a custom fetch, and `openai@6.39.1` has both SSE accumulation and an unbounded non-ok `response.text()` error-body read path; I did not run a live hostile Azure endpoint.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor-supplied real runtime proof is still required, and maintainers need either the non-ok SSE fix or an explicit partial-hardening decision before merge.

target.title: fix(azure-openai-responses): bound SSE response body reads at 16 MiB
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The azure-openai-responses provider creates OpenAI/AzureOpenAI SDK clients without a custom `fetch` option, so all SSE streaming responses from Azure OpenAI are read without any byte cap. A buggy or hostile endpoint returning `text/event-stream` with a large or never-ending body is fully buffered into memory by the SDKs internal HTTP layer — an OOM / DoS vector.

This applies the `createSdkBoundedFetch()` wrapper at `src/llm/providers/azure-openai-responses.ts:202` (OpenAI path) and `src/llm/providers/azure-openai-responses.ts:211` (AzureOpenAI path) to cap SSE response body reads at 16 MiB.

## Changes

- `src/agents/sdk-bounded-fetch.ts` — shared `createSdkBoundedFetch()` function that wraps `fetch` to cap SSE response body reads at 16 MiB (same as #97068).
- `src/agents/sdk-bounded-fetch.test.ts` — 9 tests covering the bounded fetch wrapper.
- `src/llm/providers/azure-openai-responses.ts:202,211` — inject `fetch: createSdkBoundedFetch()` into both the OpenAI and AzureOpenAI SDK constructors.
- `src/llm/providers/azure-openai-responses.test.ts` — 2 additional integration tests (6 total in file) verifying the bounded fetch.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: SSE response body reads in the azure-openai-responses provider are now bounded at 16 MiB. Both the OpenAI-compatible and native AzureOpenAI client paths are covered.
- **Real environment tested**: Linux x86_64, Node 22, vitest 4.1.8
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  `pnpm test src/agents/sdk-bounded-fetch.test.ts src/llm/providers/azure-openai-responses.test.ts --run`
- **Evidence after fix**:
  `Test Files 2 passed (2)` / `Tests 15 passed (15)`
- **Observed result after fix**: Oversized SSE response (1536 bytes body, 1024 byte cap) → stream error after 2 chunks, upstream cancelled: true, pulls limited to 2 out of 6.
- **What was not tested**: Did not test live Azure OpenAI endpoint (would not reproduce a hostile oversized body). Did not test both client paths at the HTTP level (they diverge only in constructor — the fetch wrapper is identical).

## Out of scope

Symmetric PR for openai-completions is submitted separately (A3). The transport-stream path in `openai-transport-stream.ts` is already covered by `buildGuardedModelFetch`.

## Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior change? No.
- Config/env/migration change? No.
- Security/auth/secrets/network/tool-execution change? Yes — OOM/DoS protection.
- Highest-risk area: false positive on valid large SSE payloads. 16 MiB cap aligns with existing provider bounded-read patterns.
target.author: wangmiao0668000666
